name: 13-mid-stream-5xx
description: Canonical scenario #13: mid-stream HTTP 5xx / connection-close. After the SDK has opened a 200 SSE stream and consumed message_start + 2 content_block_delta events, the emulator emits a server-error event (Anthropic: `event: error` with overloaded_error payload; OR /responses: `event: response.failed`) and closes the connection without a terminal message_stop / response.completed. Both SDKs must surface this as a typed terminal error (not a crash) — the parity claim is 'graceful handling of a mid-stream upstream failure'. Per issue body: error message text is SDK-specific phrasing and is NOT compared; the comparator runs maximally-relaxed (ignoreThrown + skipTerminalCheck + skipEventStreamCheck + skipHookOrderCheck) since the two SDKs differ structurally in how they project a mid-stream failure into their host-facing event vocabulary — the parity test that CAN be made is 'neither side crashed the harness', which the test driver's no-thrown-at-harness-level wrapper asserts via the cancelling/ignoreThrown branch. A tighter assertion (e.g. terminal:error on both sides) would require comparator-config that this card declines to add per scope-fence; documented as ambiguity call in the PR body.
prompt: Tell me a fact about networking.
systemPrompt: You are a helpful assistant. Answer concisely.

content.text: TCP relies on three-way handshakes for connection setup.
TCP relies on three-way handshakes for connection setup.
